rulings.Case Title: BRYANT FU ET AL VS. TINA YAN ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 17, 2021 line 1. CROSS DEFENDANT STELLA CHEN DEMURRER to 2ND Amended CROSS COMPLAINT Hearing Required. Peter Chao, counsel of record for Kaman Liu in action 553702 and counsel of record for Thai Ming Chiu in action 556769, to appear. Kaman Liu in action 553702 alleges that he is the assignee of a judgment in case CGC00311712 (paragraphs 8 and 9 of Cross-Complaint filed on April 26, 2017). Thai Ming Chiu in action 556769 alleges that he is the assignee of a judgment in case CGC00311712 (paragraph 7 of Second Amended Cross-Complaint filed on July 7, 2021; this paragraph is identical to the paragraphs in April 26, 2017 cross-complaint in 553702, except for the name of the assignee). By no later than 9:00 a.m. on September 17, 2021 Peter Chao to provide to the Court copies of the assignment(s) of judgment in case CGC00311712 to Kaman Liu and Thai Ming Chiu supporting the allegations in the operative cross-complaints in actions 556769 and 553702. CCP 128.7. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: XIAOZHEN WU ET AL VS. LOI TRUONG
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 17, 2021 line 6. DEFENDANT LOI TRUONG, KEITH ARMSTRONG, MY NGUYEN, SUMMER SIMPSON MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is DENIED. While not entirely clear, complaint appears to allege a cause of action for breach of contract (From Complaint PLD-C-001 CONTRACT) seeking to recover for damages to Plaintiff's property and for discrimination (Complaint for Damages 5: 11-13). Moving party seeks to strike the entire complaint arguing that the gravamen of Plaintiffs' relevant causes of action arise from Defendants' statements and participation in the prior lawsuit. While the complaint refences hiring lawyers, contacting DBI, lying 3 times in different courts, moving party does not identify any claims that arise out of these references. The Complaint seeks recovery for damage to property, discrimination and emotional distress and alleges in bold that defendants "vandalized mailbox, smoked in the room, destroyed and yanked bathtub,  deliberately waste water,  yelled "Chines Dog, Go Back to China" as form of discrimination, etc." Moving party failed to carry its burden and show that all or any claims in the complaint arise out of protected activity relying on the inapplicable "gravamen" of the complaint analysis. "At the first step, the moving defendant bears the burden of identifying all allegations of protected activity, and the claims for relief supported by them." Baral v. Schnitt (2016) 1 Cal.5th 376, 396. Moving party failed to do so. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
